bolt Sodium
Features Deployment Compare Install Docs FAQ

Game server management, simplified.

Open-source panel that runs on Node.js. No PHP, no Redis, no complexity. Just install and go.

$ git clone https://github.com/sodiumpanel/panel.git
$ cd panel && npm start
Built different

Everything you need, nothing you don't.

database

File-based database

No MySQL required. SQLite built-in for zero-config deployments that scale with you.

extension

Plugin system

Hooks, routes, middleware, and UI injection. Extend every aspect of your panel seamlessly.

key

OAuth built-in

Instant login via Discord, GitHub, Google and 5 other providers ready out of the box.

timer

2-minute install

Clone, install dependencies, and start. No complex environment setup or dependency hell.

vital_signs

Sodium Reaction

Our custom high-performance daemon written for speed. Keeps your game servers alive 24/7.

api

REST API

Full API coverage for every feature. Automate server creation, user management, and more.

Choose your setup

From a single VPS to a multi-node hosting company.

Simple / Personal

  • check_circle File-based DB
  • check_circle No Redis required
  • check_circle 1-2 server nodes
  • check_circle Up to 150 users
Scale Ready

Production / Hosting

  • check_circle MySQL / PostgreSQL support
  • check_circle Redis recommended
  • check_circle Unlimited nodes
  • check_circle High-availability clusters

Sodium vs Pterodactyl

Feature Sodium Pterodactyl
Database SQLite / MySQL / Postgres MySQL Only
Install Time ~2 Minutes ~15-30 Minutes
Dependencies Node.js Only PHP, Nginx, Redis, MySQL
OAuth Built-in (8+ Providers) Limited / Custom required
Plugin System Native Hook System Mod-heavy approach

Installation

Should take about 2 minutes.

1

Clone the repository

git clone https://github.com/sodiumpanel/panel.git
2

Install dependencies

npm install
3

Build & run

npm run build && npm start

Changelog

v1.2.0 2 days ago

The Multi-Node Update

Added native support for remote node deployment and cluster monitoring.

Step-by-step setup guides

Configuration

Tweak settings for your needs

settings

Frequently Asked Questions

Does it support Minecraft servers?

expand_more

Yes! Sodium has first-class support for Minecraft (Java & Bedrock), along with Rust, ARK, Terraria, and any game that can run in a Docker container.

Is it compatible with Wings?

expand_more

No. Sodium uses its own daemon called "Reaction". It is designed to be more lightweight and easier to configure while offering similar security via Docker.

Can I migrate from Pterodactyl?

expand_more

We are working on an official migration tool. Currently, you can manually import server files and configurations using our REST API or the Panel UI.

Does it run on Windows?

expand_more

Sodium Panel can run on Windows via Node.js for management, but we highly recommend Linux (Ubuntu/Debian) for the nodes to utilize Docker isolation properly.
